key_insight: TumorCLIP combines MRI image features with radiology-informed text prototypes to make brain tumor classification more accurate and more interpretable.
clinical_relevance: This could support more reliable MRI-based tumor subtype classification and improve confidence in model outputs for clinical workflow use.
method: Benchmark eight visual backbones with standardized optimizer and learning-rate tuning.
Use DenseNet121 as the visual encoder and fuse it with frozen CLIP-derived radiology text prototypes.
Apply a lightweight Tip-Adapter-style CLIP fusion for concept-level reasoning and few-shot/zero-shot support.
evidence_signal: On the test set, TumorCLIP reached 98.5% accuracy and a 96.9% positive semantic margin rate, outperforming the unimodal DenseNet121 baseline.
watch_item: Results come from a specific MRI classification setup, so external validation across scanners, sites, and tumor distributions is still needed before broad deployment.
paper_title: TumorCLIP: Radiology-informed vision-language alignment for interpretable MRI-based brain tumor classification
